vignettes.title: Lightning and Fire
vignettes.description: Lightning strikes a tree, causing a fire.
vignettes.notes: In this simple scenario, the fire is caused directly by the lightning strike.

vignettes.title: Backup Generator
vignettes.description: A power failure causes a backup generator to activate, supplying power.
vignettes.notes: If the primary power supply fails, the backup generator activates, ensuring electricity.

vignettes.title: Forest Fire
vignettes.description: A forest fire can be caused by either a lightning strike or an unattended campfire.

F.notes: 1 if forest fire starts, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: The forest fire occurs if either a lightning strike or an unattended campfire occurs.

vignettes.title: Teamwork on a Project
vignettes.description: Two team members, Alice and Bob, can complete a project. The project is completed if at least one of them works on it.
vignettes.notes: The project is completed if either Alice or Bob (or both) work on it.

vignettes.title: Overdetermination by Arsonists
vignettes.description: Two arsonists independently set fire to a house. The house burns down regardless of who acted first.

A1.notes: 1 if Arsonist 1 sets fire, 0 if not

A2.notes: 1 if Arsonist 2 sets fire, 0 if not

B.notes: 1 if house burns down, 0 if not

vignettes.notes: This scenario demonstrates overdetermination, where the house burns down even if only one arsonist acts.
